info.title: WorkBoard External Public API
info.description:
This is the API documentation for WorkBoard's version 1 REST API.

WorkBoard's API provides resources to allow developers to interact with user, goal, and metric data in WorkBoard. All resources can be accessed via REST with GET, POST, PUT, PATCH and DELETE requests. For GET requests, query parameters can be passed as part of the request URL. All GET, POST, PUT, PATCH and DELETE requests must include the Bearer Token authorization header with the provided token you generate.

Click here to view the [changelog](./changes.html).

Click here to view the [Open API JSON file](./openapi.json).

If you are interested in working in a demo instance, please contact your WorkBoard representative.
WorkBoard's production server API root is https://www.myworkboard.com/wb/apis.

get.summary: Get an individual user, or all users with a Data-Admin token.
get.description: **Without any parameters, this will return the user profile associated with the token. If the token is a 'Data-Admin' token, some parameters will return a different result.**

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: **Data-Admin:** Pass the user_id OR email address for the user.

parameters.name: custom_attributes
parameters.description: Return the custom attributes for a specific user. Use `custom_attributes=1` to return the values.

parameters.name: opt_filters
parameters.description: If you have provisioned your users with a unique identifier generated by your system, then you can query them with this attribute. opt_filters can be set to a JSON with the following format: opt_filters={"external_id": ["externalids"]} — where "externalids" is an array of the external ids. Example: opt_filters={"external_id": ["a123bc", "ax123b"]}

parameters.name: include
parameters.description:
• Data-Admin: Add include=org_members to return an array of all users of the organization. See `limit` and `offset` for pagination.
  • Add include=team_members to return an array of members from teams to which the current user has access.
  • Add include=org_reports to return an array of reports for the given `user_id`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: • Data-Admin: Use in combination with `include=org_members` and `offset` to return a paginated list of users of the organization. `limit` is the number of results to return.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: • Data-Admin: Use in combination with `include=org_members` and `limit` to return a paginated list of users of the organization. `offset` is the number of results to skip before returning a result.

create_at.description: The timestamp for when the user was created. Is a `string` when `include=org_members` is used.

org_user_roles.description:
When `include=org_members` is used on the `GET /wb/apis/user` endpoint, returns a comma separated numerical list of `org_roles`.
Roles are: 6 = Org Member, 7 = Org Admin, 8 = Org Root, 9 = Org Accountant, 10 = Org Data-Admin, 11 = Org HR, 12 = Org Results, 13 = Engagement Metrics, 14 = Org IT Admin, 15 = Org WorkBoard Coach

oneOf.description: Returns false if user does not have a manager.

user_reports_to_me.description: Returned on the `GET /wb/apis/user` request when `user_id` is set and `include=org_reports` is set.

is_read_only.description: Whether the user is read-only for those tenants with read-only enabled. If true, the user is a read-only user. If false, the user is a full user.

custom_attributes.description: Returned when `custom_attributes=1` is passed in the query string.

get.summary: Get all of a user's goals.
get.description: **This will return the user and a goal object with an individual object for each goal's details.**

parameters.name: user_id_path
parameters.description: The unique numerical identifier for a user in WorkBoard.

oneOf.description: Returns false if user does not have a manager.

items.description: An array of objects with the category identifier and name for the category.

metric_achieve_target.description: What has been achieved against the `metric_target`.

metric_create_at.description: The GMT timestamp value for the created at time.

metric_progress.description: The `metric_achieve_target` divided by the `metric_target` as a percent.

metric_source_from.description:
The source for metric data updates. Possible values are:
"1" - An individual user
"2" - Progress on a workstream
"3" - Another WorkBoard metric
"4" - A workstream's cycle time
"5" - A multi-user survey
"6" - Rolled up from other goals

oneOf.description: Is an empty array, if there are no goals.

get.summary: Get a team or teams that the user token is a member of. If the token is a Data-Admin token, get all teams or a single team.

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: The unique numerical id assigned to each team in WorkBoard. If you do not include this parameter, the request will return all teams to which the authenticated user belongs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: This behaves the same as the `team_id` parameter above. Do not use both `id` and `team_id`.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: This will get the team by its `external_id`. Do not use in combination with `id` or `team_id`.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Limit the teams returned based on whether the authenticated user is a team owner or member. Possible values: "owner", "member".

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: If the current authenticated user is an org data admin, pass this parameter with the value "org_teams" to return a dump of all organization teams.

parameters.name: created_at_range
parameters.description: Return teams with creation dates in the specified range. Dates must be formatted as UTC timestamps and comma-separated: startdate,enddate

parameters.name: updated_at_range
parameters.description: Return teams with last modified dates in the specified range. Dates must be formatted as UTC timestamps and comma-separated: startdate,enddate

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting point for the results returned. Permitted values are integers greater than or equal to 0. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of teams returned per page. Permitted values are integers greater than or equal to 0. The default value is 15.

team.description: Returns an array of teams if `team_id` is not passed. Returns a single object when `team_id` is passed.

items.description: The team identifiers for the dotted teams

team_members.description: Only returned on a request where a single team object is returned.

items.description: The team identifiers for the dotted teams

team_role.description: Return when the `role` parameter is used.

team_role_name.description: Return when the `role` parameter is used.

is_team_owner.description: Return when the `role` parameter is used.

post.summary: Create a new functional team or working group, and add Workstreams and team members.

team_owner.description: The existing WorkBoard user's `user_id` or email address of the user who will be the team manager.

team_name.description: The name for the team. Each team owned by an individual user must have a unique name.

team_type.description: The type of team. Permitted values are "functional" and "working_group". Note: if the parent_team_id parameter is not passed, the team will be created as a working group.

parent_team_id.description: Required for functional teams only. The id of the new team's parent in the organization's team hierarchy. If this parameter is not passed, the team will be created as a working group.

external_id.description: An immutable ID for mapping between systems.

team_members.description: The members of the team, and their team roles, as an array.

id.description: The `user_id` or existing email address of the member (for existing WorkBoard users only).

email.description: The email address of the team member (for new users only — they will receive an invitation to join WorkBoard). A new user cannot be assigned the manager `team_role`.

team_role.description: The member's role on the team. Permitted values are `manager`, `co_manager`, `exec_viewer`, `team_admin`, `member`. If you are using `manager`, please be certain this matches with the `team_owner` property above.

team_workstream.description: The Workstreams or projects that should be added to the team, as an array.

type.description: The type of Board that should be added to the team. Permitted values are "1" (Progress Board) or "2" (Custom Agile Board)

items.description: The team identifiers for the dotted teams

team_members.description: Only returned on a request where a single team object is returned.

team.description: Returns an array of teams if `team_id` is not passed. Returns a single object when `team_id` is passed.

patch.description: **CAUTION**: Delete an entire team and all its Workstreams and action items. A successful request will return a 204 code. If you are deleting a functional team, the child teams will be deleted as well.

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: The unique numerical id assigned to each team in WorkBoard. If you do not include this parameter, the request will return all teams to which the authenticated user belongs.

put.description:
Change the name of a team, add new and existing users to the team roster, change a member's role on the team, remove users from the team, or add new Workstreams. If you are using a personal access token, you must be the manager of the team to use this request.
Note: If you update a team with a new manager, the previous manager will be demoted to a co-manager.

parameters.name: team_id_path
parameters.description: The unique numerical identifier in WorkBoard for a team.

team_name.description: The name for the team. Each team owned by an individual user must have a unique name.

team_type.description: The type of team. Permitted values are "functional" and "working_group". Note: if the parent_team_id parameter is not passed, the team will be created as a working group.

parent_team_id.description: Required for functional teams only. The id of the new team's parent in the organization's team hierarchy. If this parameter is not passed, the team will be created as a working group.

team_members.description: The members of the team, and their team roles, as an array.

id.description: The `user_id` or existing email address of the member (for existing WorkBoard users only).

email.description: The email address of the team member (for new users only — they will receive an invitation to join WorkBoard)

team_role.description: The member's role on the team. Permitted values are `manager`, `co_manager`, `exec_viewer`, `team_admin`, `member`. When using the `manager` role to update a team's manager, the prior manager will become a co-manager of the team unless the `team_role` for the prior manager is specified.

team_access.description: Used together with `id` when removing an existing member from the team. Permitted value: `revoke`.

team_workstream.description: The Workstreams or projects that should be added to the team, as an array.

type.description: The type of Board that should be added to the team. Permitted values are "1" (Progress Board) or "2" (Custom Agile Board)

items.description: The team identifiers for the dotted teams

team_members.description: Only returned on a request where a single team object is returned.

get.summary: Get Goals using parameters to filter results.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Optional. A unique numerical id assigned to each goal. Include this parameter to return attributes of the specified goal only; otherwise all goals will be returned.

parameters.name: goal_owner_id
parameters.description: Either the unique user id or email address belonging to the goal owner. Include this parameter to return all goals belonging to the specified owner. (Note, if both `goal_owner_id` and `goal_id` parameters are include in the request, `goal_owner_id` will be ignored).

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: The unique numerical id assigned to each team in WorkBoard. Include this parameter to return metrics belonging to the specified team.

parameters.name: team_owner_id
parameters.description: Either the unique user id or email address belonging to the team owner. Include this parameter to return all goals belonging to the specified team owner. (Note, if both `team_owner_id` and `team_id` parameters are include in the request, `team_owner_id` will be ignored.)

parameters.name: goal_status
parameters.description: The current state of the goal; permitted values are "1" (active) or "2" (closed). If you want both closed and active goals, then set `goal_status` to the comma separated values: "1,2"

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of tags returned per page. Permitted values are integers greater than or equal to 0. The default value is 15.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Specify the starting point for the results returned. Permitted values are integers greater than or equal to 0. The default value is 0.

post.summary: Create a goal for a user in your organization, including metrics sourced from the same person. Make sure you have `Data-Admin` permission for your access token.
post.description: Create a goal for a user in your organization, including metrics sourced from the same person. Make sure you have `Data-Admin` permission for your access token.

goal_narrative.description: A description or narrative for your goal.

goal_owner.description: Either the unique user id or email address belonging to the goal owner.

goal_type.description: The goal type. Permitted values are "1" (Team goal) or "2" (Personal goal).

goal_start_date.description: The start date for the goal as a valid date string.

goal_target_date.description: The end date for the goal as a valid date string (must be later than the goal_start_date).

goal_permission.description: Comma-separated list of view permissions for the goal. Permitted values for team goals are "team", "report", "internal", "any". Permitted values for personal goals are "owner", "manager", "internal".

id.description: An array of team_id or team_name that your team members have created in the organization
An array of category_id or category_name that your team members have created in the organization

goal_metrics.description: An array of goal metrics, formatted as an array.

metric_name.description: Required The name of your metric.

metric_owner.description: Either the unique user id or email address belonging to the goal owner.

metric_source_from.description: The source of the metric. Currently, the only permitted value is "1".

metric_update_interval.description: The update interval for the metric. Permitted values "1" (daily), "2" (weekly), "3" (monthly), "4" (quarterly).

metric_counting_type.description: The relationship between the starting value and the target. Permitted values "1" (counting up), "2" (counting down), "3" (exact to target).

metric_progress_type.description: The progress calculation type. Permitted values "1" (last update), "2" (total), "3" (average).

metric_initial_data.description: The starting value for the metric.

metric_target.description: The target value for the metric.

metric_unit.description: The data type for your metric. Permitted values "1" (number), "2" (currency), "3" (percentage).

put.description: Update progress of an individual metric. If you do not include the `metric_data_id` parameter, the value will be applied to the most recent update date according to the metric update frequency set in WorkBoard.

parameters.name: metric_id_path
parameters.description: The unique metric_id to each metric.

parameters.name: force_update
parameters.description: Set to `yes` to force the update through. This allows updating closed metrics, metrics on closed objectives, metrics on objectives that have passed their target date, and applying updates to previous data points via `update_start_date` and `update_end_date` that fall outside the objective's time period.

metric_data.description: The value you want to use to update the metric.

metric_data_id.description: The unique numerical id assigned to each metric update value. If you do not include this parameter, the value passed in the `metric_data` parameter will be applied to the most recent update date according to the metric update frequency set in WorkBoard.

metric_comment.description: Include this parameter to add a comment to this metric data update.

update_start_date.description: Used in combination with update_end_date. Beginning of the date range to target which specific metric data point to update. Supports relative formats like "-46 days" or absolute dates.

update_end_date.description: End of the date range (supports same formats as update_start_date; if omitted, defaults to update_start_date). The API searches for a metric data point with a create_at timestamp within the specified date range. If found, that data point is updated. If no exact match exists, the closest data point to the start date is selected. If both dates are identical, it targets the specific data point for that date.

post.summary: Update progress on Metrics using webhooks.
post.description:
WorkBoard's webhooks allow you to post data from your external applications into WorkBoard by sending a formatted JSON payload via HTTP POST request to the secret WorkBoard URL generated for your application. Currently WorkBoard only supports incoming webhooks; outgoing webhooks will be available soon.

WorkBoard's incoming webhooks offer an easy way to update metric data in WorkBoard using a simple HTTP post.

For more advanced applications which you can customize per your requirements, you can code your own POST requests using the global hook URL which WorkBoard will generate for you.

**[Click here](https://www.myworkboard.com/wb/user/profile/edit?do=mgApp)** to get your webhook URL.

Note: If you haven't yet tried one of our pre-built connectors, you will first need to click one of the three options (Google Spreadsheet, Microsoft Excel, or From CSV) to generate your private `webhook_hash`.
